name: Exception users API
description:
The Exception users API enables you to manage which users have exception user access to your workspaces within your Anaplan tenant. You can:

  • Assign or unassign users as exception users, in specific workspaces.

  • Automate the management of exception users to assist in compliance.

Note
To use this API collection, you must enable a tenant-settings switch in the Anaplan Administration console. The switch is called Limit exception user assignment to Administration only.

URL, IP, and allowlist requirements

To set your allowlists for API calls, see: URL, IP, and allowlist requirements

resourceGroups.name: API Endpoints
resourceGroups.description:
Endpoints to manage access for exception users

  • Assign or unassign users as exception users in workspace

  • List all exception users per workspace

  • List workspaces with exception users

You can authenticate with an Anaplan token currently.
All the requests you need are in the Request headers section.

Note: You must be an active Anaplan user with the Tenant Security Admin role to use the Exception users API.

Other topics

  • Authentication

  • Request Headers

  • Error Codes

resourceGroups.name: Authentication method
resourceGroups.description:
Anaplan token authentication method

The Anaplan token authentication method uses a bearer token and has a two-step process.

Step 1 - Generate the token

You can generate the token in two ways:

  1. With a username and password
  2. With a pre-registered certificate

Both methods return the token you need to use when you call the Administration Roles API endpoints.

Step 2 - Pass the token to the Administration Roles API

User the token in Authorization header of every request.

Request header

Header Details
Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken <token from step 1> In the Authorization header value, place the word AnaplanAuthToken followed by a space and then the Anaplan Token.

Request parameters

none

Curl example

curl -X POST -H 'Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken <Anaplan token>' https://api.anaplan.com/admin/1/0/roles'

Response body

If your Authentication passes, you'll receive the response for the API you called.

resourceGroups.name: Request headers
resourceGroups.description:
All headers in this section should be sent with every request to these apis

Header Details
Authorization:
AnaplanAuthToken
* Required
Anaplan token
Accept: application/json * Optional (defaults to application/json)

This call responds with a content type of application/json.
Content-Type: application/json * Optional (defaults to application/json)

Specified the format of the request body.

Assign or unassign users as exception users in workspace

Use this endpoint to update the "exception user" status for a user in a specific workspace. Only users with the Tenant Security Administrator role can make this call.

Request

PATCH /admin/1/0/permissions/exception-users/users/{userGuid}
Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken <Anaplan token>
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "op": "assign", or "unassign",
  "workspaceGuid": "{workspaceGuid}"
}

Curl example - Assign

curl -X PATCH -H 'Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken <Anaplan token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' https://api.anaplan.com/admin/1/0/permissions/exception-users/users/{userGuid} \
--data-raw '
{
  "op": "assign",
  "workspaceGuid": "{workspaceGuid}"
}
'

Curl example - Unassign

curl -X PATCH -H 'Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken <Anaplan token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' https://api.anaplan.com/admin/1/0/permissions/exception-users/users/{userGuid} \
--data-raw '
{
  "op": "unassign",
  "workspaceGuid": "{workspaceGuid}"
}
'

Response

204 No Content: request was processed successfully

See the Error Codes table below for status messages and error scenarios.



List all exception users per workspace

Use this endpoint to list all users with "exception user" access in a workspace. All exception users, including visitors, are listed.

Request

POST /admin/1/0/permissions/exception-users/search
Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken <Anaplan token>
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "workspaceGuid": "{workspaceGuid}"
}

Curl example


curl -X POST -H 'Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken <Anaplan token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' https://api.anaplan.com/admin/1/0/permissions/exception-users/search  \
--data-raw '
{
  "workspaceGuid": "{workspaceGuid}"
}
'

Response

HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: application/json
    
{
  "response": 
  [
    {
      "workspaceGuid": "...",
      "workspaceName": "...",
      "users": [
        {
          "userGuid": "...",
          "email": "..."
        },
        {
          "userGuid": "...",
          "email": "..."
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

List workspaces with exception users

Use this endpoint to list all workspaces where a specific user has "exception user" access. Only workspaces from your tenant are returned. If the user has "exception user" access as a visitor, these workspaces are not returned.

Request

POST /admin/1/0/permissions/exception-users/search
Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken <Anaplan token>
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "userGuid": "{userGuid}"
}

Curl example


curl -X POST -H 'Authorization: AnaplanAuthToken <Anaplan token>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' https://api.anaplan.com/admin/1/0/permissions/exception-users/search  \
--data-raw '
{
  "userGuid": "{userGuid}"
}
'

Response

HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: application/json
    
{
  "response": 
  [
    {
      "workspaceGuid": "...",
      "workspaceName": "...",
      "users": [
        {
          "userGuid": "...",
          "email": "..."
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "workspaceGuid": "...",
      "workspaceName": "...",
      "users": [
        {
          "userGuid": "...",
          "email": "..."
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

resourceGroups.name: Error Codes
resourceGroups.description:
Below is a list of common error codes.

Error HTTP code Description
Invalid operation 400 Response: FAILURE BAD_REQUEST. Missing or invalid field - op. Possible values: assign, unassign.
Invalid workspace 400 Response: FAILURE BAD_REQUEST. Missing or invalid field - workspaceGuid.
Workspace is not SSO enabled 400 Response: FAILURE BAD_REQUEST. Requested workspace is not SSO enabled.
Search criteria not provided 400 Response: FAILURE BAD_REQUEST. Invalid request - search criteria not provided.
Unauthorized 401 Response: FAILURE INVALID TOKEN. Invalid token.
Forbidden 403 Response: FAILURE UNAUTHORIZED USER ACTION. Principal is unauthorized.
Trying to change status on calling user 403 Response: FAILURE_GENERIC. Operation cannot be performed on the same user as the one requesting it.
User not found or without access to workspace 400 Response: FAILURE BAD_REQUEST. Requested user not found in workspace.
Requested user not found 404 Response: FAILURE NOT FOUND. Requested user not found.
Requested workspace not found 404 Response: FAILURE NOT FOUND. Requested workspace not found.
Internal Server Error 500 Response: FAILURE GENERIC. Server failure.
